front: Work: pilgrims travel from the Tabard Inn to the tomb of Thomas Becket, telling stories along the way
Work: a wife tears pages from husband Jankin's 'book of wicked wives' and goes deaf in one ear; her tale concerns a knight given a year to learn what women most desire
back: Wife of Bath's Prologue and Tale — The Canterbury Tales (Geoffrey Chaucer)

front: Work: Palamon and Arcite fight for Emily's love; adapted from Boccaccio's Teseida
back: The Knight's Tale — The Canterbury Tales (Geoffrey Chaucer)

front: Work: the Prioress Madame Eglantine wears a pendant inscribed 'Amor Vincit Omnia' and tells an antisemitic tale of a murdered child
back: The Prioress's Tale — The Canterbury Tales (Geoffrey Chaucer)

front: Work: the author himself tells the doggerel Tale of Sir Thopas before being interrupted by Host Harry Bailey for bad verse
back: Tale of Sir Thopas — The Canterbury Tales (Geoffrey Chaucer)

front: Work: rime royale epic in which Criseyde betrays Troilus for Diomedes during the siege of Troy; first use of 'all good things must come to an end'; dedicated to John Gower
Work: dream vision in which Nature convenes birds to choose mates; narrator reads Somnium Scipionis and is guided by Scipio Africanus; 'the life so short, the craft so long to learn'
Work: an eagle carries the narrator to a temple of illustrious people; first attestation of the word 'galaxy'; words break the air and ripple
Work: first long poem, dedicated to John of Gaunt's deceased wife Blanche of Lancaster; a knight relates losing a chess game to Fortuna
back: The Book of the Duchess (Geoffrey Chaucer)

front: Author: paid a fine to beat a charge of 'raptus' against Cecily Chaumpaigne; 2022 research disputes the rape interpretation
Author: the first makar lamented by William Dunbar; Thomas Hoccleve called him 'the first founder of our fair language'
